vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P3 (Pothos) is present in the black pot with the rabbit anchor. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens; the black pot containing them appears to be empty or contains only soil/debris.

plant_audit.P1: Stable. The specimen remains in the yellow pot with no significant growth or decline observed over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P3: Stable. The white rabbit anchor remains in place. A single, thin, green sprout is visible in the soil, which may be a new shoot or incidental growth.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Container: Critical. The container is devoid of the registered botanical occupants. Only soil and scattered debris are visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White, fragmented material (possibly eggshell or perlite) is present in the P3/P4 container area.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: A single, solitary green sprout has emerged in the P3/P4 pot over the observed timeline.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Emergence of a small green sprout in the P3/P4 pot.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Sprout remains static; no further growth.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Sprout persists; no further changes in pot composition.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P1 and P3 are in a state of stasis. P2 and P4 are confirmed as systemic losses, as no foliage is present to support the registry's claims. The environment appears to be a 'dead' or 'dormant' biome with minimal biological activity.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, uncatalogued sprout in the P3/P4 pot is the primary anomaly. The lack of P2/P4 specimens suggests a failure in the initial planting or a total mortality event.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a largely dormant indoor garden. P1 and P3 remain as the only identifiable occupants. The black pot designated for P2 and P4 is currently empty of the expected species, showing only soil and some white debris. A single, small green sprout has appeared in the P3/P4 pot, which is the only sign of active development in the entire sequence. The overall health of the biome is poor, characterized by the absence of the majority of the registered flora.
